title: Mission Hero Background
scene_prompt: Cinematic documentary photograph of interconnected critical infrastructure at golden hour — a sweeping aerial view where energy transmission towers, communications relay stations, and industrial pipeline corridors converge across a vast South American highland landscape. The terrain transitions from lowland river systems to mountainous terrain, suggesting the scale of infrastructure that connects nations. Multiple infrastructure types visible in one frame: power lines cutting across valleys, a communications tower on a ridgeline, pipeline right-of-way visible through dense vegetation. Atmospheric haze separates foreground, midground, and background layers creating cinematic depth. Golden hour light from the west illuminating infrastructure metal surfaces with warm amber glow while valleys below fill with blue-green shadow. Shot on 35mm lens, f/2.8 aperture. Kodak Portra 800 film stock. Warm analog color palette — forest green (#0f4c3a) shadows filling valleys and vegetation, Safety Amber (#F4A261) highlights catching infrastructure surfaces. Fine organic film grain. Natural vignetting. Editorial documentary quality. The image feels like witnessing the connective tissue of civilization from above — the viewer understands that making this intelligent changes everything. No typography, no logos, no words, no lettering of any kind, no identifiable human faces.
palette: forest green #0f4c3a shadows, Safety Amber #F4A261 infrastructure highlights, Intelligence Navy #0A1128 distant mountains
composition: Left third reserved for text overlay. Infrastructure convergence in center-right. Depth recedes from sharp foreground infrastructure to hazy distant terrain.

title: Stakes Editorial Background
scene_prompt: Cinematic documentary photograph of a national operations center during an active event — a dimly lit command facility where multiple large wall displays show real-time feeds from different critical infrastructure systems simultaneously. One screen shows a maritime vessel tracking overlay of ocean territory, another shows an energy grid topology map with active load flows, a third shows weather satellite imagery over mountainous terrain. Operator silhouettes visible at workstations in the foreground, backs to camera, focused on the converging data streams. The scale of what they monitor is visible in the breadth of the displays — oceans, power grids, networks — all from one room. Practical lighting from the displays creates zones of blue and amber light on faces and equipment. Overhead facility lighting dimmed to reduce glare. Shot on 35mm lens, f/2.8 aperture. Kodak Portra 800 film stock. Warm analog color palette — Intelligence Navy (#0A1128) dominant with Cyber Emerald (#00D1B2) accent on active data elements and Safety Amber (#F4A261) on alert indicators. Fine organic film grain. Natural vignetting. Editorial documentary quality. The image feels like being inside the nerve center where infrastructure becomes intelligent — the viewer understands the stakes of what these operators protect. No typography, no logos, no words, no lettering of any kind, no identifiable human faces.
palette: Intelligence Navy #0A1128 dominant, Cyber Emerald #00D1B2 data accents, Safety Amber #F4A261 alert indicators
composition: Wide panoramic framing. Operator silhouettes in lower third, wall displays filling upper two-thirds. Left side slightly darker for text overlay.

title: Youth Risk Intelligence - COPRUNNA
scene_prompt: Cinematic documentary photograph of a South American government coordination center — an institutional office where multiple agency representatives work across a shared conference table covered with printed demographic maps, tablet devices showing data dashboards, and physical case folders. The room has the functional aesthetic of Latin American government buildings: terrazzo floors, fluorescent overhead lighting mixing with warm window light, agency emblems partially visible on walls. A large printed map of Ecuadorian parishes pinned to a corkboard shows color-coded risk zones in red, amber, and green. Stacked case files suggest the volume of children's records being cross-referenced. No faces visible — only hands, documents, devices, and the institutional environment that represents eighteen agencies trying to build a shared picture. Afternoon light through large windows casting long shadows across the work surfaces. Shot on 35mm lens, f/2.8 aperture. Kodak Portra 800 film stock. Warm analog color palette — forest green (#0f4c3a) in deep shadows, Safety Amber (#F4A261) in the warm window light, muted institutional colors in the environment. Fine organic film grain. Natural vignetting. Editorial documentary quality. The image feels like witnessing the moment when fragmented government systems begin to connect — the viewer understands the human stakes of data integration. No typography, no logos, no words, no lettering of any kind, no identifiable human faces.
palette: forest green #0f4c3a shadows, Safety Amber #F4A261 warm light, muted institutional tones
composition: Conference table fills lower half. Parish map and corkboard in upper right. Window light from left creating depth.

title: Galápagos Conservation Intelligence
scene_prompt: Cinematic documentary photograph of the Galápagos Islands from a patrol vessel at dawn — volcanic islands rising from dark Pacific waters, with a marine reserve patrol boat's bow and navigation equipment visible in the sharp foreground. Radar screen mounted on the bridge shows vessel positions as green dots against dark water. Beyond the bow, the distinctive silhouette of a volcanic island (flat-topped, sparse vegetation) catches the first golden light of sunrise. Open ocean stretches in every direction, communicating the vast scale of 133,000 km² of protected marine territory. Navigation instruments, radio equipment, and binoculars visible on the bridge — the tools of enforcement at sea. The water is deep blue-black with golden light beginning to illuminate wave crests. Shot on 35mm lens, f/2.8 aperture. Kodak Portra 800 film stock. Warm analog color palette — Intelligence Navy (#0A1128) in the deep ocean and pre-dawn sky, Safety Amber (#F4A261) in the sunrise hitting the volcanic peaks, Cyber Emerald (#00D1B2) glow from navigation instruments. Fine organic film grain. Natural vignetting. Editorial documentary quality. The image feels like being on the bridge of a patrol vessel responsible for one of the most important marine reserves on Earth — the viewer understands the scale of what must be protected. No typography, no logos, no words, no lettering of any kind, no identifiable human faces.
palette: Intelligence Navy #0A1128 ocean and sky, Safety Amber #F4A261 sunrise on volcanic peaks, Cyber Emerald #00D1B2 instrument glow
composition: Vessel bridge instruments in sharp foreground lower-left. Volcanic island silhouette in golden light center-right. Ocean horizon creating natural division.

title: PetroEcuador Pipeline Intelligence
scene_prompt: Cinematic documentary photograph of an Ecuadorian oil pipeline corridor cutting through Andean cloud forest — a massive steel pipeline (40cm diameter, weathered surface) running alongside a mountain road at approximately 2500 meters elevation. Dense tropical cloud forest vegetation crowds both sides of the pipeline right-of-way. A newly installed edge sensor node is visible mounted on a pipeline support structure — a weatherproof enclosure with a small solar panel and LoRa antenna, representing the IoT monitoring layer being deployed. Mist rolls through the valley below, partially obscuring the pipeline's descent toward the lowlands. The terrain dramatically drops away showing the pipeline traversing extreme elevation changes. Late afternoon light filtering through cloud cover creates volumetric rays through the forest canopy. Shot on 35mm lens, f/2.8 aperture. Kodak Portra 800 film stock. Warm analog color palette — forest green (#0f4c3a) dominant in the lush vegetation and shadows, Safety Amber (#F4A261) on the weathered pipeline metal and sensor housing, Intelligence Navy (#0A1128) in the deep valley mist. Fine organic film grain. Natural vignetting. Editorial documentary quality. The image feels like witnessing critical energy infrastructure in one of the most challenging terrains on Earth — the viewer understands why intelligent monitoring matters when manual patrol is nearly impossible. No typography, no logos, no words, no lettering of any kind, no identifiable human faces.
palette: forest green #0f4c3a dominant vegetation, Safety Amber #F4A261 pipeline metal, Intelligence Navy #0A1128 valley mist
composition: Pipeline diagonal from lower-left to upper-right. Sensor node in sharp focus mid-frame. Cloud forest depth receding into misty valley.

title: Quito Municipal Intelligence
scene_prompt: Cinematic documentary photograph of a South American city's municipal infrastructure from an elevated vantage point at blue hour — overlooking a dense urban landscape where the operational systems of a city are visible in one frame. A waste collection truck with GPS tracking antenna visible on its roof navigates a narrow street below. A municipal IoT sensor mounted on a street light pole in the foreground monitors fill levels of smart bins at an intersection. In the distance, the city's operations extend across a valley floor with mountains beyond, suggesting the scale of Quito's urban footprint. Street lights creating amber pools of light. Traffic signals cycling. The infrastructure that keeps a city running, all visible from one elevated position. Blue hour sky transitioning from deep navy above to amber at the horizon where the Andes meet the city. Shot on 35mm lens, f/2.8 aperture. Kodak Portra 800 film stock. Warm analog color palette — Intelligence Navy (#0A1128) in the blue hour sky, Safety Amber (#F4A261) in the street lighting and city glow, forest green (#0f4c3a) in vegetation and mountain silhouettes. Fine organic film grain. Natural vignetting. Editorial documentary quality. The image feels like seeing the hidden intelligence layer of a city — the viewer understands that every truck, sensor, and signal could be connected into one operational model. No typography, no logos, no words, no lettering of any kind, no identifiable human faces.
palette: Intelligence Navy #0A1128 blue hour sky, Safety Amber #F4A261 city lights, forest green #0f4c3a mountains and vegetation
composition: Elevated perspective. IoT sensor pole in sharp foreground left. City stretching to mountains in background. Waste truck visible mid-ground.
